checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the output JSON with the source PDF. The Symantec pricing table in section 1 and the Professional Services table in section 1 both accurately reflect the content on pages 1 and 2 of the source. The text regarding 'Professional Services' in section 3 correctly identifies content from page 11. While the output in section 3 contains duplicate headings and lists for 'Professional Services', this is a repetition of existing source content rather than the fabrication of new, non-existent information. The AI-generated summaries and captions are within the scope of acceptable changes. No entirely fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found.

The URLs provided in the output, such as the Symantec training link ('https://www.symantec.com/services/education-services/trainingcourses'), were checked against the source PDF (page 1) and found to be correct. No other contact information like phone numbers or emails was found in the text elements of the JSON to verify, and no fabricated information was observed.

The source document consists of 17 pages. The JSON metadata correctly identifies 17 pages. Every major section header from the source (Symantec, Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, Presidio) is represented in the output. While much of the tabular data is captured as images rather than text, the content itself is present within the output's image elements. Therefore, no entire page or section is missing.

Several tables in the output are severely truncated compared to the source. 
1. Section 4 ('Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List Part 1') contains only 2 data rows, whereas the source tables on pages 13, 14, and 15 contain a combined 43 rows of data. 
2. Section 5 ('DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form Table') contains only 1 data row, while source page 16 has 9 rows of cost items. 
3. Section 6 ('SHI CrowdStrike Price List Table') contains only 1 data row, while source page 18 has 8 rows. 
4. Section 7 ('Presidio Cisco Cost Proposal Form') contains only 1 data row, while source page 19 has 9 rows. 
In all these cases, more than 80% of the table rows are missing from the structured table element.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source pages 13-15 (Carahsoft Trellix table with 43 rows); Page 16 (DLT CrowdStrike table with 9 rows); Page 18 (SHI CrowdStrike table with 8 rows); Page 19 (Presidio Cisco table with 9 rows).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 4 Table (2 rows); Section 5 Table (1 row); Section 6 Table (1 row); Section 7 Table (1 row).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis:
There are major text and data omissions across several sections where the pipeline failed to extract structured content, providing only image placeholders. 
1. Section 2 (Sophos) contains only headers and images for pages 5 through 9 of the source, which consist of extensive pricing tables. None of this data was converted into text or table elements. 
2. Section 3 (SHI Trellix) is missing the structured data for the 'Maintenance costs', 'Training', and 'Installation' tables found on source page 11.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source pages 5-9 (Sophos pricing tables); Source page 11 (Trellix maintenance, training, and installation tables).
checks.b_evidence_output: Sections 2 and 3 do not contain structured table or text data for these pages, only headers and images.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted (primarily in Section 1 and parts of Section 3) appears to be accurate. The major issues identified in C4 and C5 represent significant structural and content failures, but the small amount of successfully parsed text does not show meaning-altering changes.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain duplicated content that is not present in the source. For example, the 'Professional Services' section in the Trellix portion appears twice in the JSON (indexes 12-14 and 15-17), but this faithfully reflects the source PDF which repeats this identical text block at the bottom of page 9 and the top of page 10 as a continuation header.
The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a consistent structure throughout the document (H2 for vendors, H3 for specific price lists, H4 for sub-sections). There are no skipped levels.

Multiple tables throughout the document are severely truncated in the conversion. The structural representation of these tables in the JSON is corrupted because the vast majority of the rows present in the source are missing from the table objects. 1. The Carahsoft Trellix table (index 15) contains only 2 data rows (Line 10 and 30) out of the 43 line items shown in the source (Pages 13-15). 2. The DLT CrowdStrike table (index 20) contains only 1 data row out of the 10 rows present in the source (Page 16). 3. The SHI CrowdStrike table (index 25) contains only 1 data row out of the 8 rows present in the source (Page 18). 4. The Presidio Cisco table (index 30) contains only 1 data row out of the 10 rows present in the source (Page 19).
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on Page 17 (Cisco) includes rows for 'Cloud Subscription', 'Customization', 'Additional Modules', 'Technical End User Doc', 'Installation', 'Technical Support', 'Training', 'Maintenance', and 'Other Costs'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding table at index 30 only contains the first data row ('Cloud Subscription/License Fee').
checks.b_evidence_location: Presidio Cisco Cost Proposal Form, Page 19, index 30

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the document is preserved, following the logical flow of the vendor sections and sub-sections as they appear in the source PDF.
The unordered list under 'Professional Services' (Trellix section, Page 11) is correctly converted into a structured list format in the JSON.

A URL is clearly visible on Page 1 of the PDF (Symantec training). While the text of the URL is captured correctly in the JSON (index 3, row 13), it is not converted into a functional, clickable hyperlink object, representing a minor loss of structural/interactive fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_location: CARAHSOFT (BROADCOM CA, INC.) SYMANTEC PRICE LIST, Page 3, index 3

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the Sophos and SHI Trellix sections is significantly compromised. In the source PDF, these sections (pages 3-9) consist of detailed pricing tables. In the converted HTML, these sections are rendered as a sequence of headings with no content (tables) between them. This fundamentally changes the layout and prevents a user from finding the primary information in the expected location.
checks.b_evidence_source: The SOPHOS section (PDF pages 3-7) and the SHI Trellix section (PDF pages 8-9) are composed of multiple detailed tables providing pricing and product information.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output for the SOPHOS and SHI Trellix sections contains only nested headings (e.g., 'Cloud Subscription/Licensing Fee', 'Professional Services and Additional Modules') with zero content or tables rendered under them.

checks.a_analysis: The 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' table is visually broken due to missing data. In the source PDF (pages 11-13), this is a continuous list of items from Line 1 to Line 43. The HTML output renders a table that only includes Line 10 and Line 30, skipping all other 41 rows. This makes the table incomplete and unusable for a sighted user looking for the full price list.
checks.b_evidence_source: Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List spanning PDF pages 11, 12, and 13, containing lines 1 through 43.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table titled 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued)' only contains two data rows: Line 10 and Line 30.
checks.b_evidence_location: Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List (Continued) section

checks.a_analysis: Multiple pages in the source PDF (3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 15) contain essential pricing information rendered as images of tables or forms. These meaningful images are completely absent from the HTML output, and there are no equivalent text or image elements representing this critical content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual tables on PDF pages 3, 4, 5, 6 (Sophos), 8, 9 (SHI Trellix), and 15 (DLT CrowdStrike) are images containing all relevant pricing data for those sections.
checks.b_evidence_output: No image elements () or converted text data corresponding to these visual tables are present in the HTML screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_location: SOPHOS, SHI Trellix, and DLT (CrowdStrike) sections

checks.a_analysis: While the 'Professional Services' text block for Trellix appears twice in the HTML output, this duplication is also present in the source PDF (bottom of page 9 and top of page 10). The HTML correctly reflects the source material's visual organization, even if the source itself contains redundant content.
